TextBlock.Text: Cerneala is a retained C# UI framework shaped for MonoGame: typed properties, logical and visual trees, templated controls, dirty queues, native Windows hosting, and backend-neutral drawing.
Describe the tree. Mutate typed state. Let the frame scheduler do only the work that became dirty.
input -> state -> measure -> arrange -> cache -> draw
Cerneala keeps a logical tree for ownership and inherited state, plus a visual tree for layout, rendering, input, and generated template parts.
Named elements remain typed. Template parts remain inspectable. Input routes reuse this structure.
Property metadata tells the scheduler whether measure, arrange, render, inheritance, or hit testing must run.
Unchanged retained command lists and shared visual order are reused instead of rebuilt for sport.
Pointer and keyboard events tunnel to the target, then bubble back through the retained route.
Describe the tree. Keep behavior in C#.
Every .cui.xml file is an additional file consumed by the incremental generator. Named elements and event hooks become compiled, typed C# with no runtime XML parser in the frame loop.

<Window Title="Cerneala">
  <Window.Resources>
    <Aspect Target="Button">
      @when IsMouseOver
      {
        Background = $Cyan;
      }
    </Aspect>
  </Window.Resources>

  <Button Name="Launch"
          Content="Launch"
          Click="OnLaunch" />
</Window>
MSBuild feeds markup to the generator.
Markup and partial class meet at build time.
Typed names, events, resources, and aspects.
A typed design system, not a bag of CSS tricks.
Aspect owns semantic tokens, component rules, templates, slots, variants, state conditions, cascade resolution, and diagnostics. It stays typed from declaration to application.
Every winner has an origin, specificity, layer, and token trace. No haunted styling séance required.
A single frame clock turns intent into trajectories, interruption, presence, and rest.
A spring wakes the shared clock.
Every channel settles in one frame.
Replay is interruptible. New intent cancels and redirects the active graph without leaking nodes.
One host frame. Dirty work only.
Relay drains a bounded FIFO snapshot, input dispatches, state settles, layout runs only where required, caches refresh independently, and the committed command stream reaches the backend.
Frame snapshots, invalidation traces, dirty-tree dumps, layout captures, routed-event routes, render-cache diagnostics, motion traces, and runtime probes make the retained engine legible.
Every page you saw is Cerneala markup, Aspect resources, retained layout, native hosting, and Motion running together.
You have reached the end of the guided tour. The lab opens a second native Cerneala Window with interactive controls.
